rationale: In the literature review, the citing paper gives a specific account of Sun, Neslin, and Srinivasan (2003): reduced-form models overestimate promotion-induced brand switching when consumers are forward looking, and a dynamic structural correction is proposed. The citation is then used to position a clear gap, namely that prior dynamic work (including this study) keeps consumption rates fixed. The 2008 paper frames its own contribution as adding endogenous flexible consumption and heterogeneity to that line of promotion-response modeling.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Sun, Neslin, and Srinivasan (2003) as prior dynamic structural evidence on forward-looking promotion response and switching-bias correction. It extends that stream by incorporating flexible consumption and decomposing promotion effects into consumption, brand switching, and stockpiling.
